claude.note: Match is 50% of 6% = 3% of $85k = ~$2,550/yr (~$212/mo on a $425/mo contribution), correct; BUT 6% of $85k is $425/mo correct. '~$440/month just in interest' on $22k at 24% is correct (~$440). However '6-7 years to pay off' with $400/mo extra is wrong — $400/mo against $22k at 24% pays off in ~6-7 YEARS only if barely above interest; actually closer, but the 'within budget' framing assumes match fits in $400 which double-counts cash flow. Math/cash-flow inconsistency docks trust.
